description_short: Gray jacket, skirt, and t shirt in silk, wool, faille, and jersey.
remark_short: Interessante è l'equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility "fashion" degli anni '40 e i canoni degli '80.
archive_description: Giacca in jersey ; monopetto; revers a uomo; spalle importanti di linea arrotondata. Vestibilità asciutta al punto vita, sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale;lunghezza ai fianchi; allacciatura a un bottone; tasche rettangolari applicate; cintura dello stesso tessuto, con profilo e fibbia ricoperti in faille di seta. Gonna in faille di seta e jersey di lana accoppiati e impunturati ; struttura a due teli,linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio e cinturetta in vita. T-shirt in jersey di lana; girocollo bordato (cm 1) in faille di seta; maniche corte; lunghezza ai fianchi;punto vita sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale; chiusura con zip sul dorso. Stola in jersey e faille abbinati e impunturati; composta da un grande rettangolo i cui lati corti sono ripiegati verso il centro e fissati in modo da creare un passaggio per le braccia.
archive_materials: Faille di seta grigio (Clerici Tessuto, Como ; art.01404, col. 413); jersey melange grigio (Lanapon/Sisan, Milano ; art. 10718, col 120, 100% lana)
archive_remark: Interessante è l’equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility “fashion” degli anni ‘40 e i canoni degli ‘80. L’essere un capo couture è dato dall’effetto di opulenza , evidenziato ai lati della silhouette e sul dietro, suggerito dalla stola.
archive_working_process: Impunture orizzontali, parallele a 1 cm, a copertura totale sui due tessuti - jersey e seta - abbinati.

description_short: Black/white/gray zigzag coat, skirt, and tunic in silk, wool, nylon, and georgette.
remark_short: The winning element here is the very Ferré masculine/feminine material mix where delicate tone-on-tone beading (tunic) contrasts playfully with a zigzag pattern (coat).
archive_description: Coat in gray/black zigzag wool. Knee-length kimono style cut with hood, capacious sleeves, slanted besom pockets (4 cm). Wool/silk edging on hood and front. Tunic in white silk georgette. Straight 7/8 cut with wide round neck, short sleeves. White jet beading all over. Skirt in wool/silk satin with gorgeous sheen. Tubular style, knee length.
archive_materials: Zigzag pattern wool (Distel, Paris; art. 960, col. 561; 100% wool), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. black; 66% wool, 27% silk, 7% nylon), silk georgette (Clerici Tessuto, Como), crystal jet
archive_remark: The winning element here is the very Ferré masculine/feminine material mix where delicate tone-on-tone beading (tunic) contrasts playfully with a zigzag pattern (coat).
archive_working_process: Allover hand embroidery with small jet beading

description_short: Printed skirt and tunic in silk, georgette, and cashmere.
remark_short: What makes the outfit distinctive is the opposition between top and bottom, namely between the geometric tunic with a quiet oriental flair and the billowy light skirt with soft…
archive_description: Tunic in cashmere knit fabric with stitched silk georgette doubling. High neck, long wide tubular sleeves, linear 7/8 cut with metal button fastening on left shoulder. Skirt in two-layer georgette. Ankle length, hip-hugging basque, skinny waistband.
archive_materials: Cashmere knit (Maglificio di Vignola, Vignola), silk georgette, silk print fabric (Sisan, Como; art. 219; des. 941, var. 6)
archive_remark: What makes the outfit distinctive is the opposition between top and bottom, namely between the geometric tunic with a quiet oriental flair and the billowy light skirt with soft pleating.
archive_working_process: Horizontal parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing; flat pleating in various depths

description_short: Gray/dove gray coat, skirt, and t shirt in silk, wool, organza, and faille.
remark_short: The elementary geo shapes of jacket and T-shirt find a perfect sense of counterpoint in the gorgeous material with rich embroidery and glossy/matte texture.
archive_description: Caban in dove gray silk organza with cornely embroidery. 7/8 length, kimono sleeves, no collar. T-shirt in dove gray silk chiffon with cornely embroidery. Hip skimming. Skirt in gray wool grisaille fabric. Straight knee-length cut. Sash in gray silk faille with 1 cm parallel topstitching.
archive_materials: Silk organza, wool grisaille (Loro Piana, Quarona Sesia), silk faille, silk chiffon; viscose ribbon
archive_remark: The elementary geo shapes of jacket and T-shirt find a perfect sense of counterpoint in the gorgeous material with rich embroidery and glossy/matte texture.
archive_working_process: Serpentine & spiral cornely embroidery with viscose ribbon (Ricamificio Damiani)

description_short: Black jacket, dress, and belt in silk, wool, nylon, and gazar.
remark_short: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré's architectural training.
archive_description: Jacket in black silk gazar. Hip-skimming circular cut with high gloss wool/silk edging. Dress in black silk gazaar with long sleeves and low-cut V neckline. Knee length. Lavish trumpet effect in front thanks to a semi-circular panel with matching cinch belt at waist.
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829 100% silk; col. black), wool/silk blend fabric (Taroni, Como; art. 17055 66% wool, 27% silk 7% nylon; col. black)
archive_remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.

description_short: Black/white/gray skirt and tunic in silk, wool, georgette, and satin.
remark_short: What's remarkable here is how an item reminiscent of a maxi pullover, semi homewear mode, can go totally glam thanks to beading creating the illusion of a shimmering tweed fabric.
archive_description: Tunica in georgette di seta grigia completamente ricamata; profondo scollo a V; manica lunga a tubo; linea a stringere al fondo; lunghezza 7/8 . Gonna in lana-seta nera; linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio.
archive_materials: Silk georgette (Taroni, Como), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. black), strass and jets in gray, white and anthracite tones
archive_remark: What’s remarkable here is how an item reminiscent of a maxi pullover, semi homewear mode, can go totally glam thanks to beading creating the illusion of a shimmering tweed fabric.
archive_working_process: Allover hand beading with tweedy effect

description_short: Black/white skirt and t shirt in silk, georgette, satin, and fur.
remark_short: The unusual use of fur, lusciously soft thanks to a special treatment, takes the concept of ultra simple tee to the next level.
archive_description: T-shirt in black breitschwanz with white silk satin collar and black silk satin bow. Skirt in two-layer black silk georgette with hip-hugging basque. Soft flat pleating, deep front slit. Ankle length.
archive_materials: Black breitschwanz fur, silk georgette (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 01031, col. black), silk satin
archive_remark: The unusual use of fur, lusciously soft thanks to a special treatment, takes the concept of ultra simple tee to the next level. Equally interesting is the contrast between superlight georgette fabric and thick rich breitschwanz fur.
archive_working_process: Fine flat pleating with even 3 cm spacing

description_short: Black/white chalkstripe evening dress, skirt, and bodice in silk, velvet, organza, and cady.
remark_short: Bordering between sleek feminine severity and slinky sari type silhouette, this dress makes a strong impact on both graphic and chromatic levels.
archive_description: Long dress with black velvet bodice and slim b&w chalkstripe silk cady skirt. Silk taffeta belt complete with decorative topstitching. Stole in chalkstripe cady with black organza on the underside. A stunning wrap construction with all the flair of a giant ribbon.
archive_materials: Black&white chalkstripe print silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 6422, des. 25, col. 1), silk velvet (Redaelli velvet; art. Prestige super; col. black), silk taffeta, silk organza
archive_remark: Bordering between sleek feminine severity and slinky sari type silhouette, this dress makes a strong impact on both graphic and chromatic levels. Another point of interest is the revisitation in evening version of a supremely mannish chalkstripe pattern.
archive_working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow

description_short: Black/white pinstripe evening dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, organza, gazar, and cady.
remark_short: The sense of illusion is clear: what appears to be an exquisitely simple strapless one-sleeve blouse is actually a stole referencing both Indian sari and Japanese origami.
archive_description: Ankle-length dress cut at waist with 6-panel bustier whalebone bodice in black & white horizontal pinstripe cady and superslim 4-panel skirt in vertical stripe cady. Medium high slit on left. Silk taffeta ribbon belt with decorative topstitching. Long slender white silk gazar stole with white silk organza lining, softly embracing arms and shoulders.
archive_materials: Black&white chalkstripe print silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 6422, des. 25, col. 1), silk gazar (Taroni, Como; art. 17028, col. 3020), silk taffeta, silk organza
archive_remark: The sense of illusion is clear: what appears to be an exquisitely simple strapless one-sleeve blouse is actually a stole referencing both Indian sari and Japanese origami. Accentuating the severity elegance further is the use of mannish chalkstripes – ever a Ferré favorite – to create a singular graphic effect essential to the designer’s rethinking of feminine chic. Another must in the Ferré fashion lexicon is the wide ribbon style belt with decorative topstitching and magnificent bow flourish.
archive_working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow

description_short: Ivory dress, cape, and belt in silk, faille, and fur.
remark_short: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan.
archive_description: Cape in ivory silk faille. 7/8 circle cut, capacious sleeves, no collar. Ivory beaver fur lining. Long dress in ivory silk faille. Slim line with two trumpet cut panels in front draping fluidly from shoulder and upper side seams. A wide belt in matching fabric holds the panels softly in place at waist yet only in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
archive_remark: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan. The sumptuous style is there in the shapes, volumes and proportions. As evident in back, the dress is for the most part slim fit. The ballgown allure comes from the gorgeous material and, best of all, from the two rich paremontures (facings) gently embracing shoulders.

description_short: Black/ivory jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, faille, and fur.
remark_short: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity.
archive_description: Jacket in boxy shape. Wide sleeves, no collar or closure. Ivory beaver fur lining. Shirt in ivory silk faille. Ample line narrowing gently toward bottom. Long sleeves. Collar and wide lapels held firmly in place at waist, no fastening. Skirt in black silk faille. Long and lavish with double construction: underneath, skinny skirt with three-button front fastening; on top, fully circular one totally open in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black & col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
archive_remark: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity. Complete with new and interesting tweaks, as in the boxy jacket versus the ballgown skirt.

description_short: Black evening dress, overskirt, and belt in silk and faille.
remark_short: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game.
archive_description: Long dress in black silk faille with trompe-l’oeil construction, namely mimicking a slimline shirtwaist worn backwards. Circular panels starting at shoulders and passing firmly under the cinch belt create an overskirt effect open in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black, 100% silk)
archive_remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, cotton, and taffeta.
remark_short: Capturing the imagination here is the opulent tartan print.
archive_description: Cape in macro tartan print silk serge with full doubling in same fabric. A-line cut, floor length. Shawl collar, capacious sleeves. Skirt in macro tartan print silk serge. Slim line, floor length, front slit. A herringbone effect in front thanks to the two-section bias cut. Back on the grain in sync with the tartan pattern. Bustier in cotton piqué with allover topstitching for a stifffish feel. Short, hip skimming style. Sash in black silk taffeta.
archive_materials: White&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk); white 100% cotton piqué, pure silk taffeta (col. black)
archive_remark: Capturing the imagination here is the opulent tartan print. The use of this pattern on the bias for the front part of the skirt adds a strong element of graphic contrast to the whole.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over bodice and sash

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, cotton, taffeta, and fur.
remark_short: The utter opulence of the cape - as in the voluminous shape complete with hint of a train, the luscious warmth of the magnificent marten lining and the glossy/matte texture of…
archive_description: Cape in macro tartan print silk serge. A-line cut, floor length (longer in back). Shawl collar, capacious sleeves. Fully reversible thanks to the doubling in rich dark marten fur. Skirt in macro tartan print silk serge. Slim line, floor length, side slit. Bustier in cotton piqué with allover topstitching for a stiffish feel. Short, (upper) hip skimming. Sash in black silk taffeta
archive_materials: White&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk); dark brown marten fur, white 100% cotton piqué, pure silk taffeta (col. black)
archive_remark: The utter opulence of the cape – as in the voluminous shape complete with hint of a train, the luscious warmth of the magnificent marten lining and the glossy/matte texture of the silk serge fabric – comes close to sparking images of royal court pomp.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over bodice and sash

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, faille, and leather.
remark_short: The architect of fashion's DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an…
archive_description: Top in black velvet with allover topstitching. Sleeveless, (upper) hip skimming. Round neck in front, total rectangular low cut in back. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve.
archive_materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
archive_remark: The architect of fashion’s DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, faille, and leather.
remark_short: Here too the architect of fashion's DNA emerges equally in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain…
archive_description: Top short and snug, (upper) hip skimming in length. Halter neck, crisscross ribbon straps down to waist in back. Allover topstitching. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back.
archive_materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
archive_remark: Here too the architect of fashion’s DNA emerges equally in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
archive_working_process: Horizonal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top and the stole

Another must in the Ferré fashion lexicon is the wide ribbon style belt with decorative topstitching and magnificent bow flourish.
archive_working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing for bow

description_short: Ivory dress, cape, and belt in silk, faille, and fur.
remark_short: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan.
archive_description: Cape in ivory silk faille. 7/8 circle cut, capacious sleeves, no collar. Ivory beaver fur lining. Long dress in ivory silk faille. Slim line with two trumpet cut panels in front draping fluidly from shoulder and upper side seams. A wide belt in matching fabric holds the panels softly in place at waist yet only in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
archive_remark: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan. The sumptuous style is there in the shapes, volumes and proportions. As evident in back, the dress is for the most part slim fit. The ballgown allure comes from the gorgeous material and, best of all, from the two rich paremontures (facings) gently embracing shoulders.

description_short: Black evening dress, overskirt, and belt in silk and faille.
remark_short: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game.
archive_description: Long dress in black silk faille with trompe-l’oeil construction, namely mimicking a slimline shirtwaist worn backwards. Circular panels starting at shoulders and passing firmly under the cinch belt create an overskirt effect open in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black, 100% silk)
archive_remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, faille, and leather.
remark_short: The architect of fashion's DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an…
archive_description: Top in black velvet with allover topstitching. Sleeveless, (upper) hip skimming. Round neck in front, total rectangular low cut in back. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve.
archive_materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
archive_remark: The architect of fashion’s DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top

description_short: Gray jacket, skirt, and t shirt in silk, wool, faille, and jersey.
remark_short: Interessante è l'equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility "fashion" degli anni '40 e i canoni degli '80.
archive_description: Giacca in jersey ; monopetto; revers a uomo; spalle importanti di linea arrotondata. Vestibilità asciutta al punto vita, sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale;lunghezza ai fianchi; allacciatura a un bottone; tasche rettangolari applicate; cintura dello stesso tessuto, con profilo e fibbia ricoperti in faille di seta. Gonna in faille di seta e jersey di lana accoppiati e impunturati ; struttura a due teli,linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio e cinturetta in vita. T-shirt in jersey di lana; girocollo bordato (cm 1) in faille di seta; maniche corte; lunghezza ai fianchi;punto vita sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale; chiusura con zip sul dorso. Stola in jersey e faille abbinati e impunturati; composta da un grande rettangolo i cui lati corti sono ripiegati verso il centro e fissati in modo da creare un passaggio per le braccia.
archive_materials: Faille di seta grigio (Clerici Tessuto, Como ; art.01404, col. 413); jersey melange grigio (Lanapon/Sisan, Milano ; art. 10718, col 120, 100% lana)
archive_remark: Interessante è l’equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility “fashion” degli anni ‘40 e i canoni degli ‘80. L’essere un capo couture è dato dall’effetto di opulenza , evidenziato ai lati della silhouette e sul dietro, suggerito dalla stola.
archive_working_process: Impunture orizzontali, parallele a 1 cm, a copertura totale sui due tessuti - jersey e seta - abbinati.

description_short: Black jacket, dress, and belt in silk, wool, nylon, and gazar.
remark_short: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré's architectural training.
archive_description: Jacket in black silk gazar. Hip-skimming circular cut with high gloss wool/silk edging. Dress in black silk gazaar with long sleeves and low-cut V neckline. Knee length. Lavish trumpet effect in front thanks to a semi-circular panel with matching cinch belt at waist.
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829 100% silk; col. black), wool/silk blend fabric (Taroni, Como; art. 17055 66% wool, 27% silk 7% nylon; col. black)
archive_remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.

Tunic in white silk georgette. Straight 7/8 cut with wide round neck, short sleeves. White jet beading all over. Skirt in wool/silk satin with gorgeous sheen. Tubular style, knee length.
archive_materials: Zigzag pattern wool (Distel, Paris; art. 960, col. 561; 100% wool), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. black; 66% wool, 27% silk, 7% nylon), silk georgette (Clerici Tessuto, Como), crystal jet
archive_remark: The winning element here is the very Ferré masculine/feminine material mix where delicate tone-on-tone beading (tunic) contrasts playfully with a zigzag pattern (coat).

remark_short: Bordering between sleek feminine severity and slinky sari type silhouette, this dress makes a strong impact on both graphic and chromatic levels.

description_short: Ivory dress, cape, and belt in silk, faille, and fur.
remark_short: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan.
archive_description: Cape in ivory silk faille. 7/8 circle cut, capacious sleeves, no collar. Ivory beaver fur lining. Long dress in ivory silk faille. Slim line with two trumpet cut panels in front draping fluidly from shoulder and upper side seams. A wide belt in matching fabric holds the panels softly in place at waist yet only in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
archive_remark: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan. The sumptuous style is there in the shapes, volumes and proportions. As evident in back, the dress is for the most part slim fit. The ballgown allure comes from the gorgeous material and, best of all, from the two rich paremontures (facings) gently embracing shoulders.

description_short: Black/ivory jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, faille, and fur.
remark_short: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity.
archive_description: Jacket in boxy shape. Wide sleeves, no collar or closure. Ivory beaver fur lining. Shirt in ivory silk faille. Ample line narrowing gently toward bottom. Long sleeves. Collar and wide lapels held firmly in place at waist, no fastening. Skirt in black silk faille. Long and lavish with double construction: underneath, skinny skirt with three-button front fastening; on top, fully circular one totally open in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black & col. 908), ivory color beaver fur
archive_remark: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity. Complete with new and interesting tweaks, as in the boxy jacket versus the ballgown skirt.

description_short: Black evening dress, overskirt, and belt in silk and faille.
remark_short: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game.
archive_description: Long dress in black silk faille with trompe-l’oeil construction, namely mimicking a slimline shirtwaist worn backwards. Circular panels starting at shoulders and passing firmly under the cinch belt create an overskirt effect open in front.
archive_materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black, 100% silk)
archive_remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, cotton, taffeta, and fur.
remark_short: The utter opulence of the cape - as in the voluminous shape complete with hint of a train, the luscious warmth of the magnificent marten lining and the glossy/matte texture of…
archive_description: Cape in macro tartan print silk serge. A-line cut, floor length (longer in back). Shawl collar, capacious sleeves. Fully reversible thanks to the doubling in rich dark marten fur. Skirt in macro tartan print silk serge. Slim line, floor length, side slit. Bustier in cotton piqué with allover topstitching for a stiffish feel. Short, (upper) hip skimming. Sash in black silk taffeta
archive_materials: White&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk); dark brown marten fur, white 100% cotton piqué, pure silk taffeta (col. black)
archive_remark: The utter opulence of the cape – as in the voluminous shape complete with hint of a train, the luscious warmth of the magnificent marten lining and the glossy/matte texture of the silk serge fabric – comes close to sparking images of royal court pomp.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over bodice and sash

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, faille, and leather.
remark_short: The architect of fashion's DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an…
archive_description: Top in black velvet with allover topstitching. Sleeveless, (upper) hip skimming. Round neck in front, total rectangular low cut in back. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve.
archive_materials: Silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, art. Yvonne super, col. black), white&black yarn-dyed silk serge with red/blue/green/black macro tartan print (Stucchi, Como; art. 4695, 100% silk), silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black)
archive_remark: The architect of fashion’s DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
archive_working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over the velvet top

description_short: Black/white/red plaid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, faille, and leather.
remark_short: Here too the architect of fashion's DNA emerges equally in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain…
archive_description: Top short and snug, (upper) hip skimming in length. Halter neck, crisscross ribbon straps down to waist in back. Allover topstitching. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. description_short: Black jacket, dress, and belt in silk, wool, nylon, and gazar.
remark_short: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré's architectural training.
archive_description: Jacket in black silk gazar. Hip-skimming circular cut with high gloss wool/silk edging. Dress in black silk gazaar with long sleeves and low-cut V neckline. Knee length. Lavish trumpet effect in front thanks to a semi-circular panel with matching cinch belt at waist.
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829 100% silk; col. black), wool/silk blend fabric (Taroni, Como; art. 17055 66% wool, 27% silk 7% nylon; col. black)
archive_remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.

description_short: Brown dress and bodice in silk, nylon, faille, and taffeta.
remark_short: This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in sublime opposition to one another.
archive_description: Dress in brown silk taffeta with decorative topstitching. Slim strapless bodice in double layer of silk faille (30 cm wide ribbon) crisscrossing neatly in front and then knotting obi style in back. Under the bustline a circular panel of topstitched silk taffeta flares beautifully outward from large fluid pleats.
archive_materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 3057, var. 824), silk faille (Clerici Tessuto; Como), nylon

description_short: Black coat, dress, and skirt in silk, cotton, nylon, and velvet.
remark_short: Here's a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man's redingote.
archive_description: Dress redingote style in black cutwork silk crêpe with black silk satin lining. Slim bodice with little lapel collar, padded shoulders, long narrow sleeves. Front metal-clasp closure with small overlay. Skirt with lovely A-line flare. Under the collar are big black organza & velvet leaf appliqués, with matching topstitched silk satin ribbon to knot below. Black organza lining for top part of the dress. Then for added fullness, the flaring skirt has a big nylon flounce on the inside.
archive_materials: Silk crepe, silk satin, silk organza, cotton velvet, nylon
archive_remark: Here’s a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man’s redingote. Distinguishing features include the silk crêpe fabric complete with meticulous cutwork: the classic eyelet effect is the amazing result of hand craftsmanship performed on the finished garment.
archive_working_process: Silk fabric all in cutwork motif

description_short: Black/white/brown jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, georgette, and satin.
remark_short: Here's another outfit capturing to perfection Gianfranco Ferré's passion for mingling masculine and feminine fashion with thoughtful elegance.
archive_description: Jacket in black grain de poudre wool. High waist, amphora shape. Radial arm construction with narrowing toward lower edge of sleeve. Round tip lapels down to waist. One-button closure. Besom pockets, plus a flap pocket on left. Knee-skimming length. Black silk crepe de chine lining. Skirt in black grain de poudre wool. Four panel construction. Slim line, knee length. Shirt in white tubular silk georgette. Kimono style sleeves narrowing at slit cuff. Mannish silk satin collar in bombé cut. Hip- hugging basque for a blousy effect. Bow in brown silk taffeta. Long and wide (5 cm) with 1 cm parallel topstiching.
archive_materials: Grain de poudre wool (Schontess, Milano; art. 799, col. black), pure silk tubular georgette (Clerici Tessuto, art. 1754, des. 110), silk satin, silk taffeta, silk crepe de chine
archive_remark: Here’s another outfit capturing to perfection Gianfranco Ferré’s passion for mingling masculine and feminine fashion with thoughtful elegance. The extralong jacket references the 19th century men’s tailcoat, where the new bombé styling in no way took away from the original design of this classic article of bourgeois clothing.
archive_working_process: Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing

description_short: Black coat, dress, and skirt in silk, cotton, nylon, and velvet.
remark_short: Here's a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man's redingote.
archive_description: Dress redingote style in black cutwork silk crêpe with black silk satin lining. Slim bodice with little lapel collar, padded shoulders, long narrow sleeves. Front metal-clasp closure with small overlay. Skirt with lovely A-line flare. Under the collar are big black organza & velvet leaf appliqués, with matching topstitched silk satin ribbon to knot below. Black organza lining for top part of the dress. Then for added fullness, the flaring skirt has a big nylon flounce on the inside.
archive_materials: Silk crepe, silk satin, silk organza, cotton velvet, nylon
archive_remark: Here’s a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man’s redingote. Distinguishing features include the silk crêpe fabric complete with meticulous cutwork: the classic eyelet effect is the amazing result of hand craftsmanship performed on the finished garment.
archive_working_process: Silk fabric all in cutwork motif

description_short: Dress.
Dress.
Brown dress and bodice in silk, nylon, faille, and taffeta.
remark_short: This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in sublime opposition to one another.
archive_description: Dress in brown silk taffeta with decorative topstitching. Slim strapless bodice in double layer of silk faille (30 cm wide ribbon) crisscrossing neatly in front and then knotting obi style in back. Under the bustline a circular panel of topstitched silk taffeta flares beautifully outward from large fluid pleats.
archive_materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 3057, var. 824), silk faille (Clerici Tessuto; Como), nylon
archive_remark: This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in sublime opposition to one another.
archive_working_process: 1 cm. parallel topstitching all along the circular panel

description_short: Black dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, nylon, and taffeta.
remark_short: Tutto si arrotonda, tutto si dilata.
archive_description: Abito – bustier composto da più elementi che si sovrappongono: il bustino è formato da due tronchi di cono che si aprono a corolla sul davanti; la parte inferiore è costituita da una gonna a ¾ di ruota su cui è cucito un elemento a mezza ruota a formare pieghe. La crinolina di nylon inserita nei profili esterni sostiene le ampiezze e favorisce un effetto plastico e tridimensionale.
archive_materials: Taffeta di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como art. 0802 - col. nero) trapunto e matelassé; duchesse di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como); crinolina di nylon.
archive_remark: Tutto si arrotonda, tutto si dilata. Il capo ondeggia nella sua ruota quasi conica con un movimento fermato nel tempo, in perfetto equilibrio di volumi e proporzioni.
archive_working_process: impunture parallele a mm10 seguono l’andamento delle forme geometriche del capo

description_short: Black/white/brown striped evening dress, skirt, and bodice in silk, velvet, faille, and gazar.
remark_short: The outstanding feature here is the artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions, so revealing once again the designer's love of hyperbole.
archive_description: Dress ballgown style in silk faille with giant stripes (black, white, fuchsia, silver). Slim strapless bodice with low-cut V neckline in back. Voluminous skirt thanks to large fluid pleats. Brown silk floral & spiral embroidery on wide black band at hip level. Black silk gazar lining. Skirt (underneath) in brown silk velvet with 1 cm topstitching. Mid-thigh length. Knotted black silk faille ribbon on waistband.
archive_materials: Silk faille in wide bands of color (Clerici Tessuto, Como; des. 2, var. 2), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como; col. brown)
archive_remark: The outstanding feature here is the artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions, so revealing once again the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how in back the large skirt rests on the short slim one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape.
archive_working_process: Complex brown silk relief embroidery; parallel topstiching with 1 cm spacing

remark_short: Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer's love of hyperbole.
archive_description: Dress with a bustier made of white cotton honeycomb piqué and a skirt in purple silk faille with black and gold stripes, with a satin weave that does not appear on the reverse side.The panels are assembled with large loose pleats in the skirt and are hollowed out from the waist up. Black silk organza lining. A topstitched satin ribbon at 0.5 cm is attached to the dress, tied in a bow at the front, over which the skirt drapes to form a drape. Nylon crinoline structure supports the shape and weight of the skirt. Brown velvet underskirt; fitted; mid-thigh length.
archive_materials: White piqué; purple silk faille with black and gold stripes (Clerici Tessuto, Como; design 3 var.4); brown silk velvet (Stucchi, Como); black silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como); nylon crinoline.
archive_remark: Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how the long full skirt rests on the short one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape. With additional support from the big satiny silk bow.
archive_working_process: Parallel topstitching at 0.5 cm, embroidery.

description_short: Black/gold evening dress, skirt, and bodice in wool, nylon, lace, and crepe.
remark_short: A dark lady of past times.
archive_description: Dress in black wool crepe with eight-part construction. Long lavishly flaring skirt with asymmetric front hem. Underneath, black nylon crinoline petticoat with three gold lamé plisse flounces. Long bodice with eight-part construction. Round neck, narrow wrist-length sleeves.
archive_materials: Wool crepe (Schontess, Milano), black nylon, gold lurex lace, gold sequins
archive_remark: A dark lady of past times. Here the chaste severity of the Victorian Age is offset by quiet elements of wicked femininity, as in the gold flounces with gilt sequins peeking out from under the skirt’s asymmetric hemline.
archive_working_process: Sunburst pleating on petticoat flounces; sequining

description_short: Black paisley evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, velvet, lace, and tulle.
remark_short: Here too the prime point of interest is the use of intarsiawork silk foulard complete with embroidery for the external layer of the skirt.
archive_description: Ballgown skirt in voluminous floor length. Exterior layer in six silk print foulards, joined together intarsia mode to form an uninterrupted pattern. Underneath, multiple circular black plisse tulle petticoats (each lifted in front and attached to waist) with lace ribbon trim. Belt in fabric with matching buckle. Top in black silk velvet. Short slim line. Spaghetti straps & piping in black tubular silk. Shawl in silk organza with partial paisley print plus lavish paillette/jet embroidery. Boxy shape.
archive_materials: Paisley print silk organza (Ratti), silk velvet (Pernet velours; art. 1112, var. 206), foulard style paisley print silk organza (Ratti), paillettes and jets, lace ribbon
archive_remark: Here too the prime point of interest is the use of intarsiawork silk foulard complete with embroidery for the external layer of the skirt.
archive_working_process: Jet/paillette encrustation of print fabric; silk foulard intarsiawork; paisley design embroidery; sunburst pleating

description_short: Black/brown paisley jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, and cashmere.
remark_short: La definizione formale di grande rigore del completo si oppone ed allo stesso tempo si compenetra dello sfarzo derivato dai colori caldi, ma soprattutto dal ricorso, volutamente…
archive_description: Giacca girocollo, senza chiusure; maniche lunghe; linea asciutta; lunghezza ai fianchi. Il tessuto di lana jacquard a disegno paisley è piazzato a scialli. I colori di base di tessitura sono bruciato, bordeaux,ocra e marrone. Il nero è creato da un sovraricamo a mano a punto catenella. Profilo davanti in zibellino tinto bordeaux. Gonna a vita alta, molto aderente; lunghezza al ginocchio. Materiale e ricamo come la giacca. remark_short: The exquisite simplicity of the jewel top makes for a perfect counterpoint to the sumptuousness of the ballgown skirt.

Belt in fabric with matching buckle. Top short sleeveless turtleneck style. In print silk with all over garnet red crystal beading. Shawl in black tulle with tone-on-tone garland-stitch hem. Boxy shape.
archive_materials: Lightweight nylon tulle, black lace ribbon, print silk organza (Sisan, Milano; art. 219, des. 1548), garnet red crystal beads

archive_description: Black silk gazaar dress with short bustier bodice and trumpet skirt. Two long asymmetric panels at back V neckline draping gently, forming a train. Silk gazar stole hanky style, folded into a triangle, with giant lapel collar attached to it cutting horizontally across the décolleté. Dark red silk velvet & organza rose vine-shoot decor adding to the beauty in back.
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, var. black)
archive_remark: While filmmaker Luchino Visconti’s masterpiece “Senso” inspires one part of the collection, this dress captures all the allure of opulent Hapsburgian fashion during the Romantic period. Offsetting the sumptuousness is the modern severity elegance of both monochrome black hue and precision-perfect asymmetry.

description_short: Gray coat in wool, crepe, and mohair.
remark_short: Si denota il fascino di Ferré per il Romanticismo sensuale e misterioso dell'Ottocento.
archive_description: Cappotto lunghezza al ginocchio, maniche a kimono tagliate, girocollo, chiusura con due bottoni rivestiti, foderato in crêpe de chine. Il capo è costruito da 5 teli cuciti ad intarsio tra loro, seguendo il motivo a volute. Il capo è completamente ricamato con fili diversi. I motivi a volute disegnano anche i davanti, il fondo del cappotto e il fondo maniche. Ulteriormente ricamato con fori che seguono il disegno.
archive_materials: Lana mohair ricamata (Agnona, art.12517, var.M65), crêpe de chine grigio scuro.
archive_remark: Si denota il fascino di Ferré per il Romanticismo sensuale e misterioso dell’Ottocento. Reso noto dal disegno del ricamo con un motivo ad ampie volute che riprendono moduli organici e scultorei che si sommano in un vortice roteante e sospeso, generando forme che plasmano il cappotto di lana.
archive_working_process: Ricamo a mano, in filo di lana grigio mélange, nero e filo di seta grigio perla; traforature su tutto il capo rinforzate con filato grigio; cuciture a intarsio.
archive_source: Cartella stampa, Collezione Alta Moda Autunno-Inverno 1988/89

description_short: Printed jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, leather, and python.
remark_short: The embroidery on the jacket - where the gracious reference to Pompier art is clear - sparks fond memories of the European salon lifestyle of the late 19th century.
archive_description: Jacket in wool flannel with thick allover wool/viscose spiral & circle embroidery. Slim hip-skimming line with round neck, long sleeves, no fastening. Extensive cord passementerie in unevenly meandering pattern, held in place here & there by wooden rings with braid covering. Tassles big and small sewn on so as to create an asymmetric effect. Shirt in wool/silk print fabric with straight line, back closure. Small Peter Pan style collar with high neckband support element. Skirt all in horizontal bands of glossy python leather. Slim fit, knee length. Belt in python leather with matching buckle.
archive_materials: Wool flannel (Lanapon, art.13702/348A), print wool/silk (Sisan, Milano; art.1095, des.1538), python leather, viscose cord passementerie, wool/viscose embroidery thread
archive_remark: The embroidery on the jacket – where the gracious reference to Pompier art is clear – sparks fond memories of the European salon lifestyle of the late 19th century. Drapery type tassles & cords define the ornamentation on an item essentially modern of cut, while also creating a sense of contrast with the (equally simple of shape) snakeskin skirt. The latter is precious in an aggressive way, thanks to the use of high-tech processes in delineating a new narrative for the basic material.
archive_working_process: Wool/viscose cornely (machine) embroidery, hand application of tassles plus viscose cord in three different sizes

description_short: Black/brown paisley coat, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, and cashmere.
remark_short: Di taglio squisitamente maschile e austero: un severo chesterfield monopetto.
archive_description: Cappotto di taglio maschile, dalla linea asciutta; monopetto a due bottoni; revers a lancia; lunghezza al ginocchio. Il tessuto di lana jacquard a disegno paisley è piazzato a scialli. I colori di base di tessitura sono bruciato, bordeaux, ocra e marrone. Il nero è creato da un sovraricamo a mano a punto catenella. Camicia di linea diritta, con chiusura posteriore, in raso di seta bordeaux, con piccolo colletto a punta stondata, sostenuto da una alta pistagna. Gonna di linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio; realizzata con 8 teli verticali in panno di lana nero; apertura con zip sul dietro. Cache-col ad effetto drappeggio irregolare, ottenuto con passaggi di fili che trattengono dall’interno il tessuto.
archive_materials: Lana jacquard a disegno cachemire (Etro, Milano; art. 639 var. 4); raso di seta (Clerici Tessuto,Como; art. 801 var. 874); panno di lana.
archive_remark: Di taglio squisitamente maschile e austero: un severo chesterfield monopetto. Il completo risulta decisamente femminilizzato dal cache- col mosso ed enfatizzato dal drappeggio, dalla calda sensualità delle tonalità, dalla raffinatezza dei sovraricami e dai macro-motivi, ricorrenti anche nei tessuti da arredamento del secondo scorcio del XIX secolo.
archive_working_process: Ricamo a mano punto catenella in filo nero, piazzato su disegno paisley jacquard.

description_short: Black/red paisley jacket, pants, and belt in silk, wool, and velvet.
remark_short: All'asciuttezza della linea della giacca-blazer fanno da contrasto sia le maniche dalla lunghezza esasperata, che necessariamente genera arricciature, sia l'ampiezza dei…
archive_description: Giacca di taglio maschile, monopetto a un bottone; revers a lancia;linea asciutta; lunghezza al fianco; maniche molto lunghe da tenere arricciate. Il capo è in raso di lana e seta stampato a disegno paisley, piazzato a foulard con cornice in tinta unita nera. Colori: bruciato, ocra,bordeaux, rosso e nero. Canotta di linea diritta in raso di seta stampato a disegni paisley (colori come giacca) girocollo, senza maniche. Pantaloni in velluto di seta stampa paisley (colori come giacca). Linea morbida creata da due pieghe che partono dalla cintura; vita alta; chiusura frontale con zip invisibile e bottone a vista. Scialle quadrato imbottito e impunturato: un lato in raso di seta, stampa a disegno paisley piazzato a foulard e l’altro in velluto di seta con stampa all over. Trapuntatura a disegno floreale .
archive_materials: raso di lana e seta stampa paisley (Ratti Donna, Como art.097/21 dis. F28501 e art. 0105/121 dis. 28501); velluto di seta stampa paisley (Ratti Donna Como art. velluto /21 dis. 59465)
archive_remark: All’asciuttezza della linea della giacca-blazer fanno da contrasto sia le maniche dalla lunghezza esasperata, che necessariamente genera arricciature, sia l’ampiezza dei pantaloni, sostanzialmente estranea ai canoni occidentali. Il motivo paisley, comune a giacca, pantaloni e scialle, assume luci diverse a causa di materiali e lavorazioni diverse nei tre pezzi.
archive_working_process: Imbottitura e trapuntatura di raso e velluto a stampa paisley abbinati.

description_short: Black/white floral coat and dress in silk and organza.
remark_short: Reproducing the structural identity of 1940s furs, the uniqueness of both materials and working processes heightens the impact of volumes and gives the coat a special sensation…
archive_description: Boxy long-sleeve crewneck 9/10th coat, buttonless. In silk organza with allover application of handcrafted white & black silk jacquard rosebuds, plus inclusion against this thick floral backdrop of feather-tipped black silk organza stalks. Slim body-hugging sleeveless high-neck tube dress, knee length. In silk organza with allover horizontal application of passementerie. This ribbon trim is sewn on with no interruptions, so to allow for the mid-back zip closure it simply switches direction and keeps on going.
archive_materials: White & black birdseye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como art. 5384/4001 var. 000/4001), silk organza, high sheen “salt & pepper” viscose passementerie
archive_remark: Reproducing the structural identity of 1940s furs, the uniqueness of both materials and working processes heightens the impact of volumes and gives the coat a special sensation of impalpable lightness. Expressly contrasting the coat's rich proportions is the slinkiness of the figure-flattering dress, second skin mode. Complete with playful glints and glossy tweed effects. All clearly strong on glamorous allure.
archive_working_process: Two allover applications: one of small handcrafted silk flowers; the other of passementerie, soutache style, sewn on horizontally non-stop (no cuts)

archive_description: Boxy long-sleeve crewneck 9/10th coat, buttonless. In silk organza with allover application of handcrafted white & black silk jacquard rosebuds, plus inclusion against this thick floral backdrop of feather-tipped black silk organza stalks. Slim body-hugging sleeveless high-neck tube dress, knee length. In silk organza with allover horizontal application of passementerie. This ribbon trim is sewn on with no interruptions, so to allow for the mid-back zip closure it simply switches direction and keeps on going.
archive_materials: White & black birdseye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como art. 5384/4001 var. 000/4001), silk organza, high sheen “salt & pepper” viscose passementerie
archive_remark: Reproducing the structural identity of 1940s furs, the uniqueness of both materials and working processes heightens the impact of volumes and gives the coat a special sensation of impalpable lightness. Expressly contrasting the coat's rich proportions is the slinkiness of the figure-flattering dress, second skin mode. Complete with playful glints and glossy tweed effects. All clearly strong on glamorous allure.
archive_working_process: Two allover applications: one of small handcrafted silk flowers; the other of passementerie, soutache style, sewn on horizontally non-stop (no cuts)

description_short: Black/white jacket and dress in silk, velvet, and cady.
remark_short: While essentially slim of silhouette, this outfit features an exquisite opulence deriving from the recherché decorative elements - all the richer at waist and wrist level.
archive_description: Jacket in white&black micro bird’s-eye silk cady. Short long-sleeve spencer cut. Lavish ripple/spiral salt&pepper silk ribbon embroidery in both flat and twofold appliqué form. Rosebuds in the same basic silk cady all along bottom hem and at wide lower edge of sleeves (diminishing upward here). Dress in black silk velvet. Short, sleeveless, low-cut rectangular neckline. Amphora shape. At midriff, black cady band with horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching. Gentle gathering at waist.
archive_materials: White&black bird’s-eye silk jacquard (Stucchi, Como; art. 5384/4001, var. 000/4001), silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk velvet (Redaelli; art. Ivonne super)
archive_remark: While essentially slim of silhouette, this outfit features an exquisite opulence deriving from the recherché decorative elements – all the richer at waist and wrist level. The short little jacket comes across as a modern-day take on the ones that back in the days of the Second Empire (1852-1870) Charles Worth combined with crinoline style skirts (so giving origin to the contemporary woman’s suit).
archive_working_process: Soutage & cornely style embroidery of narrow passementerie ribbon; application of small handmade silk flowers

description_short: Black/white plaid jacket, top, and bustier in silk, wool, cady, and fur.
remark_short: Thanks to opulent materials (dazzling crystals, fabulous fur) and artful embroidery/intarsiawork, the classic jacket is all set to hit the Gran Galà scene.
archive_description: Jacket in white&black prince of wales (glen plaid) wool with thick allover embroidery/beading. Short boxy cut. Long sleeves, no fastening. Lavish bas-relief design thanks to mix of different techniques & materials. Natural chinchilla trim. Pants in white&black prince of wales wool. Slim mannish style with tucks, pressed pleat, slant pockets. Ankle length. Bustier top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Vertical construction, (upper) hip skimming. Cummerbund in black silk cady.
archive_materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), wool (Fila, Biella; art. 16200, var. 4211), chinchilla fur (Mondialpelli, Milano), glass tube beads-crystals-paillettes
archive_remark: Thanks to opulent materials (dazzling crystals, fabulous fur) and artful embroidery/intarsiawork, the classic jacket is all set to hit the Gran Galà scene. The match with ultra mannish glen plaid pants is equally innovative, makes for an authentic conversation piece in the realm of couture.
archive_working_process: Hand embroidery with silver glass tube beads, paillettes, rosette- and teardrop-cut diamond crystals in chrome settings

description_short: Black coat, dress, and shawl in silk, velvet, and taffeta.
remark_short: The grandeur of this evening cloak enfolds in perfect unison references to typical 1950s shapes & proportions (re: American/European fashion), to the Spanish "manton de torero"…
archive_description: Gala evening coat in velvet and peau d’ange color taffeta with fine layer of padding in between. Shawl collar, wide sleeves. Trumpet line with asymmetric bombé hem forming a small train in back. Parallel topstitching in snyc with the circular construction links the three black materials. Dress sleeveless tube style, knee length. Color: black. Vertical construction with allover horizontal 1 cm parallel topstiching.
archive_materials: Silk taffeta (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 802, var. 1001), silk velvet (Pernet velours, Paris; art. 1112 /111 var 900)
archive_remark: The grandeur of this evening cloak enfolds in perfect unison references to typical 1950s shapes & proportions (re: American/European fashion), to the Spanish “manton de torero” and to the Japanese uchikake wedding kimono. Absolutely by design, the cape’s hieratic sumptuousness steals the scene from the exquisitely simple dress underneath.
archive_working_process: Allover parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing

description_short: Red skirt and t shirt in silk, georgette, and crystal beads.
remark_short: Captivating femininity takes shape on both conceptual and material levels through this shimmering jewel dress in a warm and sensual shade of red.
archive_description: T-shirt in long slim line. Sleeveless crewneck style with fine luminous effect thanks to allover garnet red crystal beading. Skirt in straight line, knee length. Necklace & bracelets torchon mode. All in garnet red crystal beads. Gloves in garnet red suede with matching silk rose appliqués.
archive_materials: Wine red silk georgette, garnet red crystal beads
archive_remark: Captivating femininity takes shape on both conceptual and material levels through this shimmering jewel dress in a warm and sensual shade of red. As for the accessories, from torchon jewelry to floral-decor gloves, they are an integral part of the outfit.
archive_working_process: Allover crystal beading (by hand) in vertical rows

description_short: Black/white/red floral skirt, shirt, and stole in silk, lace, gazar, and cady.
remark_short: Of particular interest is the chiné à la chaine silk (re stole) involving a special dye process where small groups of warp threads are tied in such a way so that they remain…
archive_description: Shirt in semi-sheer white silk gazar with extrawide lapel collar. Double breasting, two-button closure at waist. Asymmetric paisley/floral embroidery mimicking lace. Skirt in black silk cady. Slim line, high waist, ankle length. Deep slit at mid back. Belt with buckle in matching fabric. Stole long and rectangular of shape. In chiné silk taffeta with degradé dark red rose design on a black base. Black silk gazar lining, dark red rose appliqués.
archive_materials: White silk gazar (Taroni, Como), black silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), chiné à la chaine silk taffeta (Verga, Como; art 3400, var. 40, des. 16872
archive_remark: Of particular interest is the chiné à la chaine silk (re stole) involving a special dye process where small groups of warp threads are tied in such a way so that they remain color free. The warp is then set on the loom and the weaving takes place with a monochromatic weft. The little shifts that the threads undergo in the curling and tying of the warp create a vertical degradé flame effect.
archive_working_process: White-on-white silk embroidery (by hand) complete with rich relief effect

description_short: Black/red evening dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, velvet, organza, and gazar.
remark_short: While filmmaker Luchino Visconti's masterpiece "Senso" inspires one part of the collection, this dress captures all the allure of opulent Hapsburgian fashion during the Romantic…
archive_description: Black silk gazaar dress with short bustier bodice and trumpet skirt. Two long asymmetric panels at back V neckline draping gently, forming a train. Silk gazar stole hanky style, folded into a triangle, with giant lapel collar attached to it cutting horizontally across the décolleté. Dark red silk velvet & organza rose vine-shoot decor adding to the beauty in back.
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, var. black)
archive_remark: While filmmaker Luchino Visconti’s masterpiece “Senso” inspires one part of the collection, this dress captures all the allure of opulent Hapsburgian fashion during the Romantic period. Offsetting the sumptuousness is the modern severity elegance of both monochrome black hue and precision-perfect asymmetry.

description_short: Black/white/ecru striped evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, nylon, velvet, and lace.
remark_short: A main point of interest here is the use of a mannish pinstripe pattern to counterbalance both the magnificence of the delicately voluminous 19th century ballgown style and the…
archive_description: Ball gown consisting of a shaped nylon crinoline skirt, gathered and scalloped at the hem, and then machine embroidered with a pattern to imitate white lace. Starting from the waist of the skirt, 9 tiered pleated nylon tulle flounces are applied at different heights. Over this structure is placed an overskirt composed of two circular skirts: the innermost of ivory-colored mechanical Chantilly lace, and the outermost of black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes. Floor length. Separate underskirt composed of a black silk satin pencil skirt, fully doubled (not lined). Belt in black silk grosgrain with covered buckle. Fitted black silk velvet top; edges and straps in black silk tubing; length to the high hip. Square shawl in black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes, hand-hemmed.
archive_materials: Black silk gazar with white stripes (Taroni, Como; art. 9967 var. A); black nylon tulle, various weights; ecru silk Chantilly lace (Moda e guarnizioni, Milan; art. 9111); nylon crinoline; silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, Mandello Lario; art. Yvonne super 54902); black silk satin; black silk grosgrain.
archive_remark: A main point of interest here is the use of a mannish pinstripe pattern to counterbalance both the magnificence of the delicately voluminous 19th century ballgown style and the exquisite femininity of a skirt complete with beguiling glimpse of the precious layers underneath.
archive_working_process: Sunburst pleating; machine embroidery with white thread on black tulle with a lace effect.

Over this structure is placed an overskirt composed of two circular skirts: the innermost of ivory-colored mechanical Chantilly lace, and the outermost of black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes. Floor length. Separate underskirt composed of a black silk satin pencil skirt, fully doubled (not lined). Belt in black silk grosgrain with covered buckle. Fitted black silk velvet top; edges and straps in black silk tubing; length to the high hip. Square shawl in black silk gazar with white stripes, imitating pinstripes, hand-hemmed.
archive_materials: Black silk gazar with white stripes (Taroni, Como; art. 9967 var. A); black nylon tulle, various weights; ecru silk Chantilly lace (Moda e guarnizioni, Milan; art. 9111); nylon crinoline; silk velvet (Redaelli velvets, Mandello Lario; art. Yvonne super 54902); black silk satin; black silk grosgrain.
archive_remark: A main point of interest here is the use of a mannish pinstripe pattern to counterbalance both the magnificence of the delicately voluminous 19th century ballgown style and the exquisite femininity of a skirt complete with beguiling glimpse of the precious layers underneath.
archive_working_process: Sunburst pleating; machine embroidery with white thread on black tulle with a lace effect.

description_short: White/blue/navy jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, satin, and cady.
remark_short: Various decorative elements soften the impact of this outfit's simple and severe style.
archive_description: Jacket in dark blue wool satin. Round neck, long slim hip-skimming style. Strong shoulders with radial arm construction and overlay slit cuff. Slanted double besom flap pockets. Thick front buttoning with mannish flair. From upper hip to bottom hem a delicate flaring with buttons & buttonholes solely ornamental. Skirt in dark blue wool satin. Pencil cut, knee length, center slit front & back. Shirt in a natural hue of white silk cady. Short sleeves, crossover front, hip-skimming length.
archive_materials: Wool satin (Sisan, Milano; art. 12605, 100% wool, col. navy blue), silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, 100% silk, col. white)
archive_remark: Various decorative elements soften the impact of this outfit’s simple and severe style. They include the long thick row of buttons, the long glove in soft kid leather, plus the silk organza roses knotted at wrist with a topstitched ribbon.

description_short: Black/white floral evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, organza, gazar, and cady.
remark_short: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
archive_description: Long skirt in white silk cady. Spiral construction where wide band of doubled up fabric crisscrosses under bust, hugs hips, then creates a wrap-around effect all the way down. Top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Band type cut offset by diapason at mid front neckline. Bolero in white silk cady. Basically a shell with funnel sleeves. Thick application randomly all over of silk gazar and organza flowers in various shapes, sizes and colors. Small bias-cut ruffles plus cloth leaves & stems heighten the big bouquet sensation. Topstitched black ribbon knotted into a half bow.
archive_materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk organza, silk gazar
archive_remark: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
archive_working_process: Hand application of silk flowers all over

description_short: Black/white/blue solid evening dress, skirt, and t shirt in silk, wool, cotton, and organza.
remark_short: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness.
archive_description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with navy blue striping. Creating the voluminous effect are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in black wool/silk satin. Slim line, knee length. T-shirt in wool/silk with navy blue glass tube beading. Slim fit, ultra low-cut V neck, elbow-length sleeves. While the alignment of the tube beads is mostly vertical, along the neckline and the lower edge of sleeves it is horizontal, so forming a border. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
archive_materials: White cotton piqué with navy blue striping (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1036), silk organza, wool/silk satin (Taroni, art. 17055, col. black)
archive_remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Again, it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
archive_working_process: Allover glass tube beading

description_short: Blue/navy printed dress, skirt, and shirt in silk, satin, mousseline, and crepe.
remark_short: The traditionally simple notion of shirtwaist takes on a charmingly fresh flair thanks to the clever use of bias cuts and flat pleats to give the dress a swingy feel.
archive_description: Dress in crepe de chine, mannish shirtwaist style with buttoning down front and small fabric-covered buttons. Padded shoulders, shirt type cuffs. Fluid line with vertical cuts to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias. Silk mousseline doubling with flat pleats on bottom part, at same level as exterior bias-cut inserts. Skirt in dark blue silk satin. Pencil cut, knee length.
archive_materials: Crepe de chine (Distel, Paris; art. 44/154, des. 5185, 100% silk), silk print mousseline (Stucchi, Como; art. 5126, des. 40546, 100% silk)
archive_remark: The traditionally simple notion of shirtwaist takes on a charmingly fresh flair thanks to the clever use of bias cuts and flat pleats to give the dress a swingy feel. Another interesting feature is the opposition between polkadot prints: irregular on a cyan & navy blue base for the crepe de chine; small (1 cm dia) and random on a navy blue base, complete with white horizontal 8 cm parallel striping, for the silk mousseline.
archive_working_process: 1 cm flat pleating on flounce

description_short: Black/white/blue striped jacket, skirt, and bustier in silk, wool, and satin.
remark_short: Here the defining factor is the dynamic quality of forms, as if blown by a sudden gust of wind: the skirt hugs the body in back, mimics a ripple effect in front.
archive_description: Jacket in dark blue wool satin. An (upper) hip-skimming funnel shape. Lavish lapel collar with single-button closure at waist, kimono style sleeve snug in the wrist. Skirt in heavy silk herringbone-weave jacquard with regular black&white striping. Slim tubular line, knee length. A balloon effect thanks to two deeply pleated and folded back panels sewn onto the skirt at waistband level. Bustier in dark blue wool satin. Vertical strapless cut, waist length.
archive_materials: Wool satin (Sisan, Milano; art. 12605 col. blue); b&w stripe silk jacquard (Sisan, Milano; art. 4382)
archive_remark: Here the defining factor is the dynamic quality of forms, as if blown by a sudden gust of wind: the skirt hugs the body in back, mimics a ripple effect in front. The sense of opulence comes from a simple intuition: namely, doubling up the panels and playing with pleats to give volume to a flat geometric shape.

description_short: Black/white/lilac dress, skirt, and bustier in silk, cotton, crepe, and crystal beads.
remark_short: The "capote de paseo" - cape the torero uses during a bullfight - is the source of inspiration for this outfit, where telling the lead story is the contrast between the severity…
archive_description: Bustier dress faux two-piece style: strapless white cotton piqué bodice low cut in back; slim crepe de chine skirt with lilac crystal beading. Black silk 8 cm wide belt with matching vertical rectangular buckle. Cape in lilac silk gazaar. In large oval shape folded in two asymmetrically lengthwise so that the different size ruffles drape naturally.
archive_materials: Cotton piqué knit, crêpe de chine, silk gazaar (Clerici Tessuto, Como; art. 1657), crystal beads
archive_remark: The “capote de paseo” – cape the torero uses during a bullfight – is the source of inspiration for this outfit, where telling the lead story is the contrast between the severity elegance of the dress and the slinky opulence of the cape.
archive_working_process: All over crystal beading

description_short: White/red/blue polka dot evening dress in silk and chiffon.
remark_short: An apparently simple crewneck dress finds new definition thanks to the trumpet skirt effect.
archive_description: Dress in multicolor confetti print silk chiffon (fuchsia, red, yellow, turquoise, green, royal blue, white). Ankle-length siren style with round neck, straight padded shoulders and slim elbow sleeves. Soft chromatic degradé effect along hem. Two-part construction: an outer layer with vertical parallel cuts from shoulder to knee, from there wedge-shaped inserts on the bias; an inner layer with sunburst pleats peeking out from two slits at mid front and mid back, respectively.
archive_materials: Confetti print silk chiffon (Stucchi, Como; art. 5113, des. 40549, var. 001, 100% silk), silk chiffon in polkadot print (Sisan, Milano; art. 68 G/G, des. 1049, 100% silk), sequins
archive_remark: An apparently simple crewneck dress finds new definition thanks to the trumpet skirt effect. It’s a master stroke. Adding to the upbeat appeal and the swingy feel are the bias cuts, the sunburst pleats, the playful prints and the small multicolor sequins.
archive_working_process: Sequining on sunburst-pleat print fabric

description_short: Black/white/yellow dress, skirt, and bustier in silk and cady.
remark_short: Here the impression is of a sinuous female body blossoming from a bustier and an elementary skirt.
archive_description: Dress with white silk gazaar bodice, long straight black silk cady skirt. Yellow silk ribbon belt with decorative topstitching. The bodice consists of a strapless bustier with two mostly overlapping stand-up truncated cone elements set at waist. Other circular pieces are folded over and inserted in back to create a corolla or ruffle effect
archive_materials: Silk gazaar (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. white, 100% silk), silk cady (Stucchi, Como; art. 5115, 100% silk)
archive_remark: Here the impression is of a sinuous female body blossoming from a bustier and an elementary skirt. The corolla effect of the bodice derives from a masterly manipulation of geometric figures (cones and truncated cones). The dress also has the feel of a column with capital.

description_short: Black/white floral evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, organza, gazar, and cady.
remark_short: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
archive_description: Long skirt in white silk cady. Spiral construction where wide band of doubled up fabric crisscrosses under bust, hugs hips, then creates a wrap-around effect all the way down. Top in white silk cady with adhesive element. Band type cut offset by diapason at mid front neckline. Bolero in white silk cady. Basically a shell with funnel sleeves. Thick application randomly all over of silk gazar and organza flowers in various shapes, sizes and colors. Small bias-cut ruffles plus cloth leaves & stems heighten the big bouquet sensation. Topstitched black ribbon knotted into a half bow.
archive_materials: Silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691), silk organza, silk gazar
archive_remark: The contrast between sweet spring bouquet and slim spiral skirt is what captures the imagination here.
archive_working_process: Hand application of silk flowers all over

description_short: Black/white/blue solid evening dress, skirt, and top in silk, wool, cotton, and organza.
remark_short: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness.
archive_description: Long skirt in white cotton piqué with dark blue 4 cm wide stripes (20 cm spacing). In reality, forming this skirt are two fully overlapping pieces of cloth – one the piqué, the other a plain silk organza – softly gathered at the waist. Open at mid front, all the way down to hem. Short skirt in dark blue wool/silk. Slim line, knee length. Top in dark blue wool/silk with black glass tube beading. Slim fit, round neck. Totally open back with crisscrossing ribbon straps. Belt in white piqué with rectangular chrome buckle.
archive_materials: Cotton piqué in horizontal stripe print (Sisan, Milano; art. 803, des. 1055), wool/silk satin (Taroni, Como; art. 17055, col. blue), silk organza, glass tube beads
archive_remark: The volume of the long skirt captures both a once-upon-a-time majestic feel and an unknown airy lightness. Here too it’s all about the artistry of the construction process, in memory of Marcel Boucher and his enchanting creations.
archive_working_process: All over glass tube beading

description_short: Blue/navy dress, top, and bustier in wool and poplin.
remark_short: This creation highlights the designer's unique ability to fashion clothes as if pieces of sculpture.
archive_description: Dress in dark blue wool, knee length. Two-tier construction: a slim tube style on the bottom, a sleeveless halter style on top. Adding to the beauty of the bodice part is a high collar open in front down to waist. It folds back to form a strapless bustier with three-button fastening midway. A similar folding process marks the otherwise open-back dress.
archive_materials: Wool poplin (Agnona, Biella; art. 32480/1; col. blue 02509, 100% wool)
archive_remark: This creation highlights the designer’s unique ability to fashion clothes as if pieces of sculpture. The trick is in the apparently simple sartorial solutions – bordering on the origami technique and as such in the finest Ferré tradition.

description_short: White/blue/navy jacket, skirt, and shirt in silk, wool, satin, and cady.
remark_short: Various decorative elements soften the impact of this outfit's simple and severe style.
archive_description: Jacket in dark blue wool satin. Round neck, long slim hip-skimming style. Strong shoulders with radial arm construction and overlay slit cuff. Slanted double besom flap pockets. Thick front buttoning with mannish flair. From upper hip to bottom hem a delicate flaring with buttons & buttonholes solely ornamental. Skirt in dark blue wool satin. Pencil cut, knee length, center slit front & back. Shirt in a natural hue of white silk cady. Short sleeves, crossover front, hip-skimming length.
archive_materials: Wool satin (Sisan, Milano; art. 12605, 100% wool, col. navy blue), silk cady (Taroni, Como; art. 16691, 100% silk, col. white)
archive_remark: Various decorative elements soften the impact of this outfit’s simple and severe style. They include the long thick row of buttons, the long glove in soft kid leather, plus the silk organza roses knotted at wrist with a topstitched ribbon.
